- `DNS:*.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local`

This is perfect! The wildcard `*.elasticsearch.svc` and `*.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local` cover:
- `es-http.elasticsearch.svc` (for the HTTP service)
- `es-transport.elasticsearch.svc` (for the transport service)

The wildcard `*.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local` only matches one level deep, so it wouldn't actually cover `es-0.es-transport.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local` since that's nested deeper. But `*.elasticsearch.svc` does cover the single-level subdomains like `es-transport.elasticsearch.svc` and `es-http.elasticsearch.svc`, and similarly `*.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local` covers one level below that domain.

For the inter-node transport connections using pod hostnames like `es-0.es-transport.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local`, those sit two levels deep and wouldn't match the wildcard. ---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: es-config
  namespace: elasticsearch
data:
  elasticsearch.yml: |
    cluster.name: es-cluster
    node.name: ${NODE_NAME}
    network.host: "0.0.0.0"
    discovery.seed_hosts: "es-transport"
    cluster.initial_master_nodes: "es-0,es-1,es-2"
    xpack.security.enabled: "true"
    xpack.security.enrollment.enabled: "false"
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled: "true"
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.verification_mode: certificate
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.key: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/tls/tls.key
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.certificate: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/tls/tls.crt
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.certificate_authorities: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/tls/ca.crt
    xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled: "true"
    xpack.security.http.ssl.key: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/tls/tls.key
    xpack.security.http.ssl.certificate: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/tls/tls.crt
    xpack.security.http.ssl.certificate_authorities: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/tls/ca.crt
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: es-transport
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  clusterIP: None
  selector:
    app: elasticsearch
  ports:
    - name: transport
      port: 9300
      targetPort: 9300
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: es-http
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  selector:
    app: elasticsearch
  ports:
    - name: https
      port: 9200
      targetPort: 9200
---
apiVersion: policy/v1
kind: PodDisruptionBudget
metadata:
  name: es-pdb
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  minAvailable: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: elasticsearch
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: es
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  serviceName: es-transport
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: elasticsearch
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: elasticsearch
    spec:
      securityContext:
        runAsUser: 1000
        fsGroup: 1000
      initContainers:
        - name: increase-vm-max-map
          image: busybox
          command: ["sysctl", "-w", "vm.max_map_count=262144"]
          securityContext:
            privileged: true
      containers:
        - name: elasticsearch
          image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.11.1
          env:
            - name: NODE_NAME
              valueFrom:
                fieldRef:
                  fieldPath: metadata.name
            - name: ELASTIC_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: elastic-password
                  key: password
            - name: ES_JAVA_OPTS
              value: "-Xms512m -Xmx512m"
          ports:
            - containerPort: 9200
              name: https
            - containerPort: 9300
              name: transport
          resources:
            requests:
              memory: "1Gi"
              cpu: "500m"
            limits:
              memory: "1Gi"
              cpu: "1000m"
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
            - name: config
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/elasticsearch.yml
              subPath: elasticsearch.yml
            - name: tls
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/tls
              readOnly: true
          readinessProbe:
            exec:
              command:
                - sh
                - -c
                - |
                  curl -s --cacert /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/tls/ca.crt \
                    -u "elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD}" \
                    https://localhost:9200/_cluster/health | grep -qE '"status":"(green|yellow)"'
            initialDelaySeconds: 60
            periodSeconds: 15
            timeoutSeconds: 10
            failureThreshold: 6
  volumeClaimTemplates:
    - metadata:
        name: data
      spec:
        accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
        resources:
          requests:
            storage: 5Gi

content.thinking:
The error is `failed to resolve host [es-transport]` — Elasticsearch can't resolve the headless service `es-transport`. This is a DNS issue.

The headless service is named `es-transport` in the `elasticsearch` namespace, but the pods can't resolve it as just `es-transport`. They need to use the full DNS name `es-transport.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local` or the shorter form `es-transport.elasticsearch`.

Wait - but in Kubernetes, pods in the same namespace should be able to resolve services by their short name `es-transport`. Let me check if the DNS is working.
 
Actually, I'm wondering if the headless service doesn't have any endpoints yet because the pods aren't ready. For headless services, Kubernetes includes pod IPs in DNS when the pod is Running and either the pod is Ready or `publishNotReadyAddresses: true` is set on the service. Since the pods are Running, I need to check if that flag is configured.

Ah, that's the issue—without `publishNotReadyAddresses: true`, the headless service won't include the pods in DNS resolution since they're not Ready yet. This is a classic chicken-and-egg problem with Elasticsearch in Kubernetes. I need to add that flag to the headless service definition.

content.text: DNS fails because the headless service has no endpoints — pods are running but not yet Ready, and `publishNotReadyAddresses` is false. This is the classic ES bootstrap chicken-and-egg. Adding `publishNotReadyAddresses: true` to the headless service.

---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: es-config
  namespace: elasticsearch
data:
  elasticsearch.yml: |
    cluster.name: es-cluster
    node.name: ${NODE_NAME}
    network.host: "0.0.0.0"
    discovery.seed_hosts: "es-transport"
    cluster.initial_master_nodes: "es-0,es-1,es-2"
    xpack.security.enabled: "true"
    xpack.security.enrollment.enabled: "false"
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled: "true"
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.verification_mode: certificate
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.key: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/tls/tls.key
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.certificate: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/tls/tls.crt
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.certificate_authorities: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/tls/ca.crt
    xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled: "true"
    xpack.security.http.ssl.key: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/tls/tls.key
    xpack.security.http.ssl.certificate: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/tls/tls.crt
    xpack.security.http.ssl.certificate_authorities: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/tls/ca.crt
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: es-transport
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  clusterIP: None
  selector:
    app: elasticsearch
  ports:
    - name: transport
      port: 9300
      targetPort: 9300
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: es-http
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  selector:
    app: elasticsearch
  ports:
    - name: https
      port: 9200
      targetPort: 9200
---
apiVersion: policy/v1
kind: PodDisruptionBudget
metadata:
  name: es-pdb
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  minAvailable: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: elasticsearch
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: es
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  serviceName: es-transport
  replicas: 3
  podManagementPolicy: Parallel
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: elasticsearch
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: elasticsearch
    spec:
      securityContext:
        runAsUser: 1000
        fsGroup: 1000
      containers:
        - name: elasticsearch
          image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.11.1
          env:
            - name: NODE_NAME
              valueFrom:
                fieldRef:
                  fieldPath: metadata.name
            - name: ELASTIC_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: elastic-password
                  key: password
            - name: ES_JAVA_OPTS
              value: "-Xms512m -Xmx512m"
          ports:
            - containerPort: 9200
              name: https
            - containerPort: 9300
              name: transport
          resources:
            requests:
              memory: "1Gi"
              cpu: "500m"
            limits:
              memory: "1Gi"
              cpu: "1000m"
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
            - name: config
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/elasticsearch.yml
              subPath: elasticsearch.yml
            - name: tls
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/tls
              readOnly: true
          readinessProbe:
            exec:
              command:
                - sh
                - -c
                - |
                  curl -s --cacert /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/tls/ca.crt \
                    -u "elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD}" \
                    https://localhost:9200/_cluster/health | grep -qE '"status":"(green|yellow)"'
            initialDelaySeconds: 30
            periodSeconds: 15
            timeoutSeconds: 10
            failureThreshold: 20
      volumes:
        - name: config
          configMap:
            name: es-config
        - name: tls
          secret:
            secretName: es-http-tls
  volumeClaimTemplates:
    - metadata:
        name: data
      spec:
        accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
        resources:
          requests:
            storage: 5Gi
